title: ByteWolf World Memory Claim v0.1
description: One evidence-backed claim about the world: what was seen, by which sensor, when, how certainly, and until when it may be believed. World memory is separate from the personal memory the post-turn hook writes; see docs/world-memory-v0_1.md.

contract_version.description: Pins the meaning of every field below. A consumer that does not know this version must reject the claim rather than guess.

subject.description: The place, object, or mission a claim is about, e.g. 'marker:red-pad' or 'mission:9f2c'. A subject names a thing in the world, never a person.

category.description: Deliberately narrow. There is no person, face, or identity category: a detection may not become an identity claim.

statement.description: What is claimed, in one bounded sentence. It is evidence for a human or a planner to read, never a command.

evidence.description: Without all four fields there is no claim: an unsourced, undated, unquantified, never-expiring belief is exactly what this store exists to prevent.

source.description: What produced the claim, e.g. 'camera:down_rgb', 'lidar_2d_v2', or 'mission-artifact'. Free text for audit, never for dispatch.

observed_at.description: RFC 3339, UTC, with an explicit offset. When the world was observed, not when the claim was written.

expires_at.description: After this instant the claim is no longer believed. It must be later than observed_at: a claim that never expires would outlive the world it described.

confidence.description: How strongly the producer believes the statement. Zero is not admissible: a claim nobody believes is not evidence.

artifact.description: Repository-relative path to the recorded run, frame, or scan the claim was derived from, so a reader can go back to the raw evidence.

position.description: Where the subject is, when that is known. Absent means unknown, never zero.
